antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: They lead with muscle, macros, recovery, and performance discipline. PEP should make protein feel social, light, refreshing, and occasion-ready instead.

antiReferences.name: Clinical supplement brands
antiReferences.why: They rely on rational benefit stacks and problem-solution health language. PEP needs pleasure, taste, belonging, and social confidence.

antiReferences.name: Meal replacement drinks
antiReferences.why: They suggest solo utility, heaviness, and routine nutrition. PEP is a light social drink, not a substitute meal.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision only. It is a different product and brand world; do not borrow positioning, claims, or identity assumptions from it.

antiReferences.name: Kids’ juice or candy soda cues
antiReferences.why: They undermine premium hospitality credibility and make the drink feel childish rather than adult, social, and stylish.

antiReferences.name: Moralising abstinence-led drink brands
antiReferences.why: They define the occasion by restriction. PEP should define it by feeling good, having fun, and enjoying a better social drink.

audience: Socially active adults who want a more exciting drink option when they are out.
People who care about taste, presentation, and what the drink says about the occasion.
Consumers who like functional benefits but do not want a gym-coded drink in a bar, restaurant, hotel, festival, or date setting.
Hospitality customers choosing between cocktails, beer, wine, mocktails, mixers, and premium soft drinks.
Style-conscious groups looking for something refreshing, fun, and premium enough to order publicly.
brandFitRules: Judge fit by occasion first: bars, dinners, festivals, dates, rooftops, hotels, and group rituals are active-direction moments.
Do not sort brands by alcohol content; sort by occasion, drink format, and social brand behaviour.
A strict in-line peer should share the social-drink mindset and a ready-to-serve or directly orderable beverage role.
Use positioningFitTypeMismatch for brands that nail the social ritual but differ by drink format, such as spirits-style bottles, cider, mixers, or cocktail formats.
Reject references that lead with gym performance, macros, recovery, bodybuilding, or disciplined routine.
Protein should appear as a smart benefit, never as the dominant personality of the brand.
Taste, refreshment, and occasion should sit above nutrition in the hierarchy for this direction.
Premium must stay fun and approachable, not formal, cold, or exclusionary.
The brand system must stretch across Coconut Mango and future flavours without making one flavour family the defining trait.
Never treat drinkpep.com as the same brand as pep-drink.com.

designPatterns: Builds a drink world around mood and occasion rather than basic refreshment.
Uses ritual-led storytelling that makes the serve feel special.
Feels designed for social discovery and lifestyle relevance.
Keeps functional benefits present while allowing the experience to lead.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong fit: social functional beverage with a ready-to-drink role close to PEP’s ambition, though PEP must remain distinctly protein-led and lighter in social expression.
inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: Closest strategic peer for making function feel social, experiential, and premium. Useful for how to make a benefit-led drink feel like part of a night out rather than a supplement.

inLineBrands.category: Mocktails and adult social soft drinks
designPatterns: Cocktail-inspired serving cues suited to bars and restaurants.
Adult social positioning without feeling clinical or gym-oriented.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong fit: directly competes in adult social drink occasions and is ordered as a ready social beverage, even though it is not a protein drink.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Mocktails
inLineBrands.why: A useful in-line reference for adult social refreshment, hospitality relevance, and flavour-led presentation in social settings.

designPatterns: High confidence in a bar-style occasion.
Simple, premium ordering logic around serve and flavour.
Strong sense of occasion and immediate drinkability.
Feels suited to events, hotels, and social venues.
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Good fit by occasion and ready-to-drink social format; not a protein drink, but highly relevant to PEP’s competitive set in out-of-home ordering.
inLineBrands.name: On The Rocks Cocktails
inLineBrands.why: Useful for premium RTD confidence and bar-inspired social cues. PEP should borrow the sense of ready-to-order quality, not the cocktail category codes directly.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only if helpful for website structure, such as a clear flavour grid, product detail rhythm, and simple conversion path.
Do not use as a core brand-positioning reference unless its social occasion energy is intentionally adapted.
Keep any borrowing structural only: navigation clarity, product modules, and flavour comparison mechanics.

oneLiner: PEP is the premium protein drink for going out: refreshing, flavour-led, social, and made to feel good while having fun.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: hydration mix format and routine replenishment behaviour.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional-protein / functional hydration mindset.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is too rational, electrolyte-led, and performance-adjacent for PEP’s social ordering role.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: BCAA and energy drink format points toward sport and performance.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its main logic is energy, training, and athletic function rather than premium social experience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Partial product-theme overlap through protein, but weak social-brand fit because it is novelty and performance-coded.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs mainly to functional-protein through its protein-first novelty proposition.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: The brand logic is fusion and fitness curiosity rather than a polished, flavour-led hospitality ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: functional hydration bottle format and vitamin-led rationale.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional hydration and routine wellness.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is closer to daily functional replenishment than an exciting drink to order when out.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak-to-medium: canned soda format is relevant, but gut-health lifestyle logic is not PEP’s core.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade / prebiotic soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It leads with wellness and healthier soda replacement rather than premium social ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium by social serve possibility, but primarily a botanical soft-drink lifestyle upgrade.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade with a natural, subtle, everyday-premium mindset.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more restrained and wellness-natural than PEP’s fun, festival-ready social direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak-to-medium: soda can format is relevant, but prebiotic benefit is the category driver.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade / prebiotic soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its centre of gravity is health-conscious everyday soda, not out-of-home social ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak-to-medium: modern soda format is relevant, but wellness-soda behaviour dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade / prebiotic soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more daily lifestyle refreshment than premium social drink ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium: kombucha can work in cafés and casual social settings, but the fermentation and wellness craft cues lead.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Yaya Kombucha
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source, but closer to lifestyle wellness and craft refreshment than active social direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its authenticity and organic craft world are useful context, but PEP should not become a kombucha-style wellness drink.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium: botanical soda and kombucha can appear in social alternatives, but format and craft-fermentation logic differ.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Walker Brothers
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source, but closer to lifestyle craft beverage and wellness refreshment.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more nuanced craft refreshment than high-energy premium social protein drink.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium: kombucha is a refreshing beverage, but its core ritual is wellness and everyday balance.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It speaks to health-focused lifestyle habits rather than the excitement of ordering a social drink out.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium: canned infused refreshment is relevant, but its mood is calmer and everyday wellness-led.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade / relaxation beverage.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its promise is calm and decompression; PEP’s active direction is more social, fun, and event-ready.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium-to-strong by RTD party format, but its source positioning is lifestyle upgrade rather than PEP’s protein-led social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Source marks it as lifestyle upgrade, with party and outdoor use cases overlapping the social direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful as inspiration, but not the core model because PEP needs to own protein refreshment in social settings rather than cocktail convenience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Medium: social serve relevance, but spirits and wine alternative format differs from PEP.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade with premium mindful socialising cues.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more about recreating classic drink formats than building a new protein-refreshment social ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for active social direction despite direct protein relevance.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Juuz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is a functional protein drink reference, but its likely purchase logic is protein utility and refreshment rather than premium out-of-home social ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: vitamin drink format and benefit-led hierarchy.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional drinks and vitamin supplementation.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its use case is daily functional enhancement, not social experience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak-to-medium: functional soda format is relevant, but benefit-led wellness soda logic dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: more fiz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade / functional soda.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is closer to healthy soda replacement than a premium protein drink for social settings.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker format fit: bottle-and-mix ritual rather than a ready protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Lyre's
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strongly matches the social occasion, but its category behaves like a spirits cabinet and cocktail-building system.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirits-style format, not PEP’s light protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Excellent reference for premium social ritual, serve culture, hospitality credibility, and making the drink feel adult and occasion-worthy. Borrow the sophistication of the ritual and the confidence of the presentation, not the exact category codes.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker format fit: mixed-drink component rather than a standalone protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Seedlip
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strongly social and premium, but rooted in spirits-style mixing and bar craft.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirits-style bottle format and mixer-dependent ritual.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for showing how a functional-adjacent drink can earn premium hospitality placement through ritual, serve language, and a refined sense of occasion.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker format fit: cider is a different core beverage format from protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Rekorderlig
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Strongly social and refreshing, especially for outdoor, festival, and group moments.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Cider format, not protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the outdoor social energy, easy refreshment cues, flavour-led hierarchy, and relaxed premium feel. It is close to the desired festival-ready social world even though the drink format differs.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Weaker format fit: often used as a mixer or classic soft serve rather than a protein-led standalone drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Royal Club Ginger Ale
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Matches adult social occasions but plays more in mixer and classic soft-drink behaviour.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Mixer format, not protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for adult refreshment, bar compatibility, and the sense that a soft drink can still feel mature and orderable in hospitality.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Partial fit: RTD social format is relevant, but cocktail logic is different from protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ole cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source marks it as lifestyle upgrade, though it overlaps strongly with social occasions such as parties and outdoor events.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Cocktail format rather than protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Borrow the celebratory energy, clear flavour hierarchy, convenient serve logic, and festive premium feel. Do not borrow category cues that make PEP seem like a cocktail brand.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Partial fit: strong social serve relevance, but category logic centres on recreating classic drink formats.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ish
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source marks it as lifestyle upgrade, with strong overlap in dinner parties and socialising.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Spirits, wine, and cocktail alternative format rather than protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for premium social credibility, modern restraint, and adult serve cues. Borrow the hospitality seriousness while keeping PEP more playful and refreshing.

positioningStatement: For socially active adults who want a better drink option when they are out, PEP is a premium protein drink that turns refreshment into a fun social ritual. Unlike gym-coded protein drinks or basic soft drinks, PEP leads with flavour, lightness, and occasion — with protein as a smart benefit that helps people feel good and have fun.

visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe
visual.mood: Warm, social, high-energy, polished, refreshing, expressive, and premium-but-approachable. The brand should feel like something people want to order, hold, photograph, and share in out-of-home social settings.
visual.photography: Show PEP in bars, dinners, festivals, rooftops, terraces, hotels, dates, and group moments. Prioritise hands, serves, chilled refreshment, table scenes, movement, and real social atmosphere. Avoid making people look like they are training, dieting, or taking a supplement.
rejectLooks: Clinical supplement layouts with dense claims and rational benefit stacks.
Gym-coded protein design built around muscle, recovery, discipline, or transformation.
Meal-replacement seriousness or heavy nutrition-first hierarchy.
Overly sparse luxury that feels stiff, silent, or inaccessible.
Childish soft-drink styling that reduces premium credibility.
Generic wellness-soda minimalism with no social energy.
Any identity cues copied directly from competitor logos, label systems, or signature assets.

voice.tone: Playful, confident, social, crisp, flavour-forward, and premium-but-approachable. Copy should feel like an invitation to a good moment, not a supplement explanation.
